title: Scope backlog-add auto-commit to explicit queue-submits, surface merge-in-progress skips
action: Per D1: add a --queue-submit boolean flag to handleBacklogAdd (bee.mjs ~L2533), default false when omitted. commitBacklogRow (bee.mjs ~L2502) must only attempt the git add+commit when queue-submit is true; when false, keep appendJsonl's write but skip straight to {committed:false, sha:null} without invoking git at all. Per D2: before commitBacklogRow's existing add/commit sequence runs (only reachable when queue-submit is true), resolve the real git-dir via `git rev-parse --git-dir` (reuse runBacklogGit) and check for a MERGE_HEAD file there (not a hardcoded .git/MERGE_HEAD -- linked worktrees point .git elsewhere). If present, skip the add/commit attempt and return {committed:false, sha:null, commit_skipped_reason:'merge_in_progress'}. handleBacklogAdd's returned text must append a visible warning suffix when commit_skipped_reason is 'merge_in_progress' (e.g. ' (auto-commit skipped: merge in progress)'), distinct from today's silent no-suffix skip for every other reason. Update lib/command-registry.mjs's backlog.add entry: add 'queue-submit' to parameters.properties (type boolean, description explaining the human-queue-submit vs agent-self-observation distinction per D1), and extend the description text to state the new default-false gating contract. Add test coverage in test_cli_cells.mjs: (a) a real git-initialized fixture (new helper or extend makeStateRepo) proving --queue-submit omitted/false never invokes git and returns committed:false with no commit created; (b) --queue-submit true performs the scoped commit and returns committed:true + commit_sha, touching only .bee/backlog.jsonl; (c) a MERGE_HEAD present in the fixture's git-dir with --queue-submit true returns commit_skipped_reason:'merge_in_progress' and the text output carries the warning suffix, without attempting a commit. After the template edit, run the project's established onboard sync (onboard_bee.mjs --apply) so .bee/bin/bee.mjs and .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs mirror the change byte-for-byte -- confirm both copies match post-sync.

truths: bee backlog add with no --queue-submit flag appends the row to .bee/backlog.jsonl but performs zero git operations and returns committed:false
bee backlog add --queue-submit performs the existing scoped git add+commit against .bee/backlog.jsonl only and returns committed:true with a commit_sha

bee backlog add --queue-submit while a merge is in progress (MERGE_HEAD present) returns committed:false, commit_skipped_reason:'merge_in_progress', and the CLI text output visibly names the skip reason
.bee/bin/bee.mjs and .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs match skills/bee-hive/templates/ byte-for-byte after sync

artifacts.substantive: commitBacklogRow gates the git add+commit on queue-submit and detects MERGE_HEAD before attempting it; handleBacklogAdd parses --queue-submit and surfaces commit_skipped_reason in text
backlog.add entry documents the queue-submit flag and the new default-false gating contract

key_links: handleBacklogAdd passes the parsed queue-submit flag into commitBacklogRow
test_cli_cells.mjs's new git-fixture tests call the real bee.mjs backlog add dispatcher (runBeeBacklog), not a mocked commitBacklogRow
prohibitions: No change to appendJsonl's unconditional row-append -- every valid add still lands in .bee/backlog.jsonl regardless of queue-submit
No change to commitBacklogRow's scoped pathspec discipline (still git add/commit -- against .bee/backlog.jsonl alone, never -A or a plain commit)
No new commit_skipped_reason values beyond 'merge_in_progress' (other failure paths stay silent committed:false, per D2 YAGNI scope)

trace.outcome: commitBacklogRow gates the git add+commit on a new --queue-submit flag (default false, D1) and detects a merge in progress via the resolved git-dir + MERGE_HEAD before attempting it (D2); handleBacklogAdd parses --queue-submit and surfaces commit_skipped_reason:'merge_in_progress' with a visible warning suffix in the text output; command-registry.mjs documents the flag; 3 new real-git-fixture tests added to test_cli_cells.mjs
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_cells.mjs", "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "test_cli_cells.mjs: 'bee.mjs backlog add with --queue-submit omitted never invokes git and returns committed:false with no commit created' (new, real git-repo fixture)",
    "test_cli_cells.mjs: 'bee.mjs backlog add --queue-submit performs the scoped commit, returns committed:true + commit_sha, touching only .bee/backlog.jsonl' (new)",
    "test_cli_cells.mjs: 'bee.mjs backlog add --queue-submit with a merge in progress returns commit_skipped_reason:\"merge_in_progress\" and the text output carries the warning suffix, without committing' (new)"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Pre-change commitBacklogRow(root, line) (git show HEAD^:skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs lines ~2502-2531, prior to this cell's commit) took no queueSubmit parameter and attempted the scoped git add+commit unconditionally whenever inside a git work tree, with no MERGE_HEAD check at all. Against that code the new 'queue-submit omitted' test would fail: it would return committed:true and advance HEAD instead of the asserted committed:false with HEAD unchanged; the merge-in-progress test would also fail since no commit_skipped_reason existed and the commit would be attempted (and fail) inside a merge with no distinguishing signal.",
  "verification_run": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS, 88 suite(s), all green (recorded via cells verify on this cell's trace); targeted node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_cells.mjs -> 27 passed, 0 failed; node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs -> 249 passed, 0 failed (registry bijection + backlog.add example both green with the new queue-submit parameter/description)",
  "deliberate_exceptions": []
}
